perturbation.original_text: The initial production period shall be for a period of two (2) months to commence on November 4, 1998 (the "Production Period"), and the remaining term of this Agreement shall be for a period of twenty four (24) months to commence on the tentative launch date of January 4, 1999, unless terminated earlier as provided herein (the "Promotion Period"), (The Production Period and the Promotion Period shall be collectively referred to as the "Initial Term").
perturbation.changed_text: The initial production period shall be for a period of two (2) months to commence on November 4, 1998 (the "Production Period"), and the remaining term of this Agreement shall be for a period of eighteen (18) months to commence on the tentative launch date of January 4, 1999, unless terminated earlier as provided herein (the "Promotion Period"), (The Production Period and the Promotion Period shall be collectively referred to as the "Initial Term").
perturbation.explanation: Changing the 'Promotion Period' duration from twenty-four (24) months to eighteen (18) months introduces a contradiction in the definition of the 'Initial Term'. This change contradicts the referenced of the Initial Term in 'Section 4. Exclusivity', where iVillage agrees to an exclusivity agreement for the Initial Term, now creating ambiguity over the period of exclusivity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the Initial Term of this Agreement, iVillage agrees that Ford shall be the exclusive automobile manufacturer sponsor and advertiser throughout the Network, with respect to entities whose primary business is that of an automotive manufacturer and/or retailer. For purposes of this Agreement, the term "retailer" shall refer to an entity which sells new and/or used vehicles. In addition, in the event that iVillage desires to form a sponsorship relationship with an automobile rental company during the term of this Agreement, iVillage shall notify Hertz and provide Hertz with an opportunity to enter into such a relationship with iVillage, on not less favorable terms than those offered to any other automobile rental company. Once presented with an opportunity, Hertz shall have five (5) business days in which to accept or reject such terms. If iVillage does not receive Hertz's acceptance or rejection of such within the allotted time, iVillage shall deem Hertz's silence as rejection. The terms of any such relationship shall be mutually determined by the Parties. Notwithstanding the foregoing, FMC shall, on a non-exclusive basis, 3 be permitted to offer Ford Motor Credit car financing products related to the purchase of Ford vehicles.
perturbation.justification: The change from twenty-four (24) months to eighteen (18) months for the 'Promotion Period' directly impacts the 'Initial Term' definition. The exclusivity agreement in the contradicted text relies on the Initial Term, now creating ambiguity and a direct contradiction over the period of exclusivity. This corresponds to a clear instance of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction', and would most likely cause confusion, unenforceability, or a legal issue within the document.

perturbation.original_text: During the Promotion Period, iVillage will deliver a minimum of [*] advertising impressions, in an equal proportion each month. Subject to reasonable written notice to iVillage, FMM may request a reasonable reallocation of impressions as determined by FMM.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Promotion Period, iVillage will use its best efforts to deliver a minimum of [*] advertising impressions, with no guarantee of equal monthly distribution. Subject to written notice to iVillage, FMM may suggest a reallocation of impressions, subject to iVillage's final approval.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'will deliver a minimum of' with 'will use its best efforts to deliver a minimum of' introduces ambiguity, making the delivery of advertising impressions non-binding. This is then compounded by removing the need for equal proportion each month. Adding the fact that iVillage has final approval in any reallocation suggested by FMM. This contradicts the text in 'Section 2.C.(iii)', which describes the penalties or remedies FMM has in place if the impressions are not delivered. Now creating conflict, if iVillage doesn't deliver the impressions, there isn't any breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (iii) In the event that iVillage fails to deliver the advertising impressions during the Promotion Period, FMM shall have the option of either (a) extending the Initial Term of this Agreement for an additional three (3) month period to "make good" the undelivered impressions or (b) requiring iVillage to refund to FMM an amount equal to [*] for each [*] impressions which were not delivered.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies iVillage's obligation from a firm commitment ('will deliver a minimum of') to a 'best efforts' basis, removing the guarantee of equal monthly distribution. Crucially, it also grants iVillage final approval on reallocation, which was previously FMM's right with reasonable notice. This directly contradicts Section 2.C.(iii), which outlines FMM's remedies if iVillage fails to deliver impressions. Since the changed text weakens iVillage's delivery obligation and FMM's power to reallocate, there is no longer a breach of contract if iVillage fails to deliver impressions, rendering FMM's remedies unenforceable and creating a clear in-text contradiction based on the 'Ambiguities' definition.

perturbation.original_text: iVillage agrees to execute any and all necessary further documents that FMC may reasonably request to fully vest the intellectual property rights related to the Materials in FMC and, if requested, to reasonable assist FMC in registering such rights in the name of FMC.
perturbation.changed_text: iVillage agrees to consider executing further documents that FMC may request regarding the intellectual property rights related to the Materials, but retains the right to refuse if such documents are deemed unreasonable by iVillage. iVillage may assist FMC in registering such rights, at iVillage's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'agrees to execute any and all necessary further documents' to 'agrees to consider executing further documents' and adding the stipulation that iVillage can refuse if it deems the documents unreasonable, weakens the original obligation. Additionally, it changes 'reasonable assist' to 'may assist...at iVillage's sole discretion', creating a significant contradiction with 'Section 7. Proprietary Rights', where iVillage assigns to FMC all rights in the content, design, and intellectual property for the Materials, now creating uncertainty about the completeness of the IP rights transfer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon execution and delivery of this Agreement, iVillage assigns to FMC all right, title and interest in and to the content, design and intellectual property, rights created specifically for and unique to the Bridge Site, advertising units, and other promotional elements set forth in this Agreement (collectively, the "Materials"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, iVillage expressly retains all right, title and interest in and to the programs and software that are used in connection with the creation and operation of, but are not created specifically, for and unique to the Materials (the "iVillage Proprietary Materials"). FMC acknowledges and agrees that the iVillage Proprietary Materials are used by iVillage in creating and developing Web sites for itself and other parties. FMC further acknowledges and agrees that iVillage will be using certain licensed programs and software owned by third parties for portions of the development and creation of the Materials and that FMC will not acquire any right in or to those copyrighted materials. iVillage agrees to consider executing further documents that FMC may request regarding the intellectual property rights related to the Materials, but retains the right to refuse if such documents are deemed unreasonable by iVillage. iVillage may assist FMC in registering such rights, at iVillage's sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens iVillage's obligation to fully vest intellectual property rights in FMC. The original text states iVillage 'agrees to execute any and all necessary further documents,' while the changed text states iVillage 'agrees to consider executing further documents' and can refuse if deemed unreasonable. Also it changes 'reasonable assist' to 'may assist...at iVillage's sole discretion'. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which says 'iVillage assigns to FMC all right, title and interest in and to the content, design and intellectual property' making it a strong in-text contradiction, as the completeness of the IP rights transfer is now uncertain.
